carbon_policy_signal.item: EU methane enforcement rules loosened; oil companies gain exemption scope, reducing compliance cost and abatement incentive for gas producers.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Lower abatement compliance cost reduces investment in methane-mitigation projects; fewer EUA offsets issued; EUA floor pressure via lower sectoral decarbonization hurdle.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale as of 26 Dec 2025 (137 days old); clean-dark spread stale as of 31 Dec 2025 (132 days old). Current coal price and fuel-switch economics likely outdated; rely on TTF, DE power, and EUA (current as of 11–12 May 2026) for trade sizing.
risk_flags: Coal at 8th-percentile (USD 96/t) — data stale as of 26 Dec 2025 (137 days old); clean-dark spread at 50th pctile likely undervalues current thermal dispatch.
GB power at 89th pctile vs. DE 58th pctile; 13.5% GB weekly decline and 11.15% daily drop suggest supply relief or demand fade — cross-border flows may widen spread if sustained.
Renewable share down 13.45% daily and -21.57% monthly (52nd pctile); wind deployment delays risk further contraction, raising fossil dispatch call into H2 2026.
Russia sanctions on shadow fleet and banking escalating; if LNG routing narrows, TTF upside risk extends beyond current 61st-pctile baseline.

base.summary: TTF consolidates 61st pctile; GB-DE spread moderates; coal dispatch supportive but not urgent.

downside.summary: Libya gas ramp succeeds; EU methane rules loosen supply costs; Iran war de-escalates; TTF gives back geopolitical premium.

upside.summary: Russia shadow-fleet sanctions escalation closes LNG routing; Libya ramp delayed; TTF scarcity premium widens.

themes: Geopolitical supply disruption (Iran, Russia sanctions) vs. EU policy softening on methane
Coal historically depressed; fuel-switch economics favor gas over coal despite TTF strength
GB power premium (89th pctile) vs. DE power (58th pctile) — cross-border arbitrage tightening
Wind deployment delays risk renewable baseload shortfall; fossil dispatch upside persists
top_drivers: Iran war and Russia sanctions escalation sustain TTF premium (61st pctile, +4.73% daily) despite EU methane-rule softening that would otherwise moderate supply-cost pass-through.
Coal at 8th-percentile (stale data) and renewable share down 21.57% monthly signal persistent fuel-switch headroom favoring gas over coal; fossil dispatch stays in-the-money.
GB power at 89th pctile vs. DE 58th pctile reflects domestic supply or demand divergence; cross-border arb may tighten spread if GB supply relief sustains.
top_takeaway: Iran war supply disruption and Russia sanctions escalation support TTF (61st pctile, +4.73% daily), while coal at 8th pctile signals fuel-switch headroom; GB power at 89th pctile reflects supply premium.

watchlist.metric: Libya gas production ramp-up (Italy supply target)
watchlist.why: Italy pursuing Libyan pipeline as Iran-war hedge; supply shortfall would extend TTF scarcity premium into weeks-ahead curve.

watchlist.metric: EU methane enforcement final rules (compliance exemptions)
watchlist.why: Looser rules reduce gas-producer abatement cost, potentially moderating TTF upside but lowering EUA via fewer offset incentives.

watchlist.metric: Russian shadow fleet / banking sanctions (LNG routing impact)
watchlist.why: Escalation risk to EU supply flows; each tranche narrows LNG-to-EU capacity and supports fuel-switch premium in power curve.

watchlist.metric: Portugal fossil-fuel phase-out roadmap & renewable auctions 2026–2027
watchlist.why: Policy acceleration signals long-term decarbonization but near-term wind delays create baseload gap; coal/gas dispatch upside if renewable deployment stalls.

watchlist_dated.name: EU methane enforcement final implementation
watchlist_dated.why: Exemption scope clarification will signal gas supply cost trajectory and EUA abatement incentive strength.

watchlist_dated.name: Further Russia sanctions announcement (shadow fleet/banking)
watchlist_dated.why: Each round tightens LNG routing; TTF scarcity premium at risk if announcements confirm multi-month channel closures.
